commit.message:
chore(vscode): replace deprecated workspace settings keys (#40823)

## **Description**

This PR updates the repository workspace settings to use the current VS
Code JavaScript and TypeScript setting keys.

The existing `.vscode/settings.json` file still referenced deprecated
language-specific keys. That creates avoidable warnings in VS Code and
makes the workspace configuration look stale even though the underlying
behavior is still valid.

This change replaces the deprecated keys with their modern `js/ts.*`
equivalents while preserving the existing workspace behavior:
- keep import module specifiers set to `relative`
- keep the workspace TypeScript SDK pointed at
`node_modules/typescript/lib`

[![Open in GitHub
Codespaces](https://github.com/codespaces/badge.svg)](https://codespaces.new/MetaMask/metamask-extension/pull/40823?quickstart=1)

## **Changelog**

CHANGELOG entry: null

<!-- ## **Related issues** -->

<!-- Fixes: -->

## **Manual testing steps**

1. Open the repository in VS Code.
2. Open `.vscode/settings.json` and confirm the deprecated
`javascript.preferences.importModuleSpecifier`,
`typescript.preferences.importModuleSpecifier`, and `typescript.tsdk`
keys are no longer present.
3. Confirm the workspace now uses
`js/ts.preferences.importModuleSpecifier` and `js/ts.tsdk.path` without
showing deprecation warnings.

<!-- ## **Screenshots/Recordings** -->

<!-- ### **Before** -->

<!-- Not applicable. -->

<!-- ### **After** -->

<!-- Not applicable. -->

## **Pre-merge author checklist**

- [ ] I've followed [MetaMask Contributor
Docs](https://github.com/MetaMask/contributor-docs) and [MetaMask
Extension Coding
Standards](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/.github/guidelines/CODING_GUIDELINES.md).
- [ ] I've completed the PR template to the best of my ability
- [ ] I’ve included tests if applicable
- [ ] I’ve documented my code using [JSDoc](https://jsdoc.app/) format
if applicable
- [ ] I’ve applied the right labels on the PR (see [labeling
guidelines](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/.github/guidelines/LABELING_GUIDELINES.md)).
Not required for external contributors.

## **Pre-merge reviewer checklist**

- [ ] I've manually tested the PR (e.g. pull and build branch, run the
app, test code being changed).
- [ ] I confirm that this PR addresses all acceptance criteria described
in the ticket it closes and includes the necessary testing evidence such
as recordings and or screenshots.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: metamask-extension
repository.description: :globe_with_meridians: :electric_plug: The MetaMask browser extension enables browsing Ethereum blockchain enabled websites

commit.message:
fix: exclude SSE requests from ServiceWorker fetch interception

The SW's fetch handler was wrapping all requests including the /events
SSE stream. When the long-lived SSE connection dropped, the catch block
returned a 503 instead of letting the browser handle reconnection,
causing "ServiceWorker encountered an unexpected error" in Firefox.

Now SW skips any request with Accept: text/event-stream so SSE
connections pass through unmodified.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: expose cert IP SANs in /info endpoint

Adds a certIps array to the /info JSON response so users can verify
which IP addresses the TLS certificate is valid for without needing
to inspect the cert manually.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set descriptive CN on self-signed cert and force regeneration

Android's CA install dialog shows the cert's CN as the "from" label.
Without a meaningful CN it shows "null" or "AgentBridge" (too terse).

- Change CN from "AgentBridge" to "AgentBridge Local Network"
- Set country to FI
- Add certHasExpectedSubject() check alongside certHasCaFlag() so any
  existing keystore with the old CN is deleted and regenerated on next
  startup, making the dialog show "AgentBridge Local Network"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add CA BasicConstraints flag to self-signed TLS cert

Android's CA certificate installer rejects certs without BasicConstraints
CA:TRUE and shows "private key required" instead of the proper CA trust
prompt.

- Add `-ext BC:critical=ca:true` to the keytool genkeypair command
- Add `certHasCaFlag()` check so existing keystores without the flag are
  automatically deleted and regenerated on next startup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: serve cert as PEM and document CA cert install path for Android

- `/cert.crt` now serves PEM format with `application/x-pem-file` MIME type
  and filename `agentbridge-ca.pem`. DER binary triggered Android's VPN/app
  certificate installer which requires a private key; PEM avoids that.
- RELEASE_NOTES.md and plugin.xml updated with step-by-step Android and iOS
  CA certificate installation instructions, including the "don't just tap
  the file" caveat that causes the "Private key required" error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: correct 1.16.0 release notes

- Codex uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, not ACP
- Remove 'Android' specificity from PWA section
- Remove internal bug fixes that weren't user-visible features

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: update 1.16.0 release notes, fix thinking chip test, remove stale integration tests

- Rewrote RELEASE_NOTES.md 1.16.0 section with user-focused highlights
- Added 1.16.0 change-notes to plugin.xml for the JetBrains Marketplace
- Updated chat-controller.test.js: 'Thought complete' → 'Thought' to match
  actual ThinkingChip render output (chip always shows '💭 Thought')
- Deleted McpToolsIntegrationTest and McpToolsExtendedTest — broken by
  psi-bridge.json format change, unused in CI, and unmaintained

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add /cert.crt download endpoint and include LAN IPs in TLS certificate SAN

- New GET /cert.crt handler serves the self-signed certificate in DER format
  with application/x-x509-ca-cert content type so Android prompts to install it
- collectLocalIpv4Addresses() enumerates all up, non-loopback IPv4 interfaces
  and adds them to the keytool SAN at cert generation time
- certCoversAllIps() checks whether the stored cert covers all current LAN IPs;
  if not, the keystore is deleted and regenerated automatically
- sslKeyStore field stores the loaded KeyStore so handleCert() can read the
  certificate without re-opening the file on every request
- Update RELEASE_NOTES.md with full 1.16.0 changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: reformat executeJs call in ChatConsolePanel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add missing override modifier to updateToolCall and remove unused jsStatus

- Add `override` to `updateToolCall` in ChatConsolePanel to satisfy ChatPanelApi
- Remove default parameter values from the override (defaults belong on the interface only)
- Remove unused `jsStatus` local variable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: fix queued message positioning and cancel button rendering

- Add _insertMsg() helper that places new messages before any queued ones,
  ensuring queued messages always stay at the bottom of the chat
- Call _moveQueuedToBottom() after loading history batches to restore correct order
- Fix cancel button not rendering: it was missing from the built JS due to
  a stray "Commit and push changes" line that caused a build syntax error
- Fix queued message timestamp label hidden: add .show class to message-meta
  so the "⏳ Queued for end of turn" label is visible

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Show full history dropdown on empty input for login fields

Adds showAllOnEmpty prop to AutocompleteInput (default false, fully
backwards-compatible). When true, the dropdown opens with all suggestions
even when the input is empty — useful for fields pre-populated from
history where filtering to the persisted value hides other options.

Applied to Server and Database fields on ConnectionDialog only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Clamp traversal/display depth when slider maximum is reduced

When maxTraversalAndDisplayDepth is saved, atomically clamp the
persisted traversalDepth in the same settingsStore.update() call.
A useRef-guarded useEffect in AppShell clamps local displayDepth and
traversalDepth state immediately so sliders snap visually on change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 'Back to Login' button to sidebar bottom

Wires to existing DISCONNECT IPC channel. Clears all session state
(graph records, schema, search history) and returns to ConnectionDialog.
Button is pinned to the sidebar bottom above the Defaults Folder section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: drag from inside modal to overlay no longer dismisses dialog

Introduces a shared ModalOverlay component that centralises the
modal-overlay backdrop and a drag-safe close pattern. The overlay only
fires onClose when both mousedown and click originated on the overlay
itself, so text-selection drags that overshoot the dialog boundary are
ignored. All four existing modals (TraversalSettings, SearchPopup,
RecordPicker, VisibilitySettings) are refactored to use ModalOverlay.
Any future modal using ModalOverlay with onClose gets this behaviour
automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: proportional hint input widths when sidebar is expanded

Wraps each AutocompleteInput in a flex container whose flex-grow is
proportional to the number of characters typed. With nothing typed both
inputs share space equally (flex 1:1). As the user types a long table
name or column name the inputs expand proportionally, so e.g. typing
'GteBooking' (10) and 'GBK_ReferenceNumber' (19) gives roughly a 10:19
width split, reducing wasted space.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop previous traversal before clearing store on re-search

Prevents stale RECORDS_ADDED IPC events from bleeding into a fresh search.
TraversalService.stop() now awaits the runLoop promise, guaranteeing no
further onDepthComplete callbacks fire after stop() resolves. App.tsx
invokes stop-traversal before recordStore.clear() on every new search.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Simplify RULE-022 shell entity condition logic

Remove unnecessary business_type guard in the shell indicator check.
has_no_address already requires physical_address to be explicitly
present in the case data (key must exist), so the condition is clean
without the extra guard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: aml-sar-ai-system2
repository.description: AI-powered system that analyses suspicious financial alerts and automatically generates Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) using RAG and LLMs.

commit.message:
Phase 2: Add rules engine enhancements, ORM models, config, tests, and deployment files

- Enhance src/rules.py:
  - Add confidence_score (float, default 0.5) and recommendation (str, default '')
    fields to RuleResult dataclass
  - Update all 17 existing rules with appropriate confidence scores and actionable
    recommendations
  - Fix RULE-015 (TMS alert) to be informational-only (triggered=False) since
    TMS origin alone is not a standalone suspicious indicator
  - Add RULE-018 rule_layering_chains: 3+ distinct countries in transactions
  - Add RULE-019 rule_trade_based_laundering: max/min amount ratio > 10x + intl
  - Add RULE-020 rule_dormant_account_activity: >180 days inactivity + high volume
  - Add RULE-021 rule_rapid_account_turnover: account_age_days < 90 + high volume
  - Add RULE-022 rule_shell_entity_indicators: shell/holding/nominee business types
  - Add RULE-023 rule_beneficial_owner_mismatch: ubo_mismatch flag
  - Add RULE-024 rule_cross_border_frequency: >5 international transfers
  - Add AMLRuleEngine class with run_all_rules, get_triggered_rules,
    calculate_overall_risk, and explain_decision methods

- Fix src/output.py: add dataclass-aware JSON serializer so RuleResult objects
  in the suspicion dict are properly serialized to the audit log file

- Create src/models.py: SQLAlchemy ORM models (User, Case, SAR, ExtractionResult,
  AuditLog, ApprovalWorkflow, CaseAttachment, SystemConfig) with indexes and
  relationships for full case lifecycle management

- Create config.py: centralised Pydantic Settings configuration with DB, API,
  JWT, LLM, RAG, rule threshold, feature flag, and CORS settings

- Update requirements.txt: add pydantic-settings, sqlalchemy, psycopg2-binary,
  python-jose, passlib, python-multipart, alembic, pytest, httpx, python-dotenv

- Create Dockerfile: Python 3.11-slim image with uvicorn API server

- Create docker-compose.yml: api + PostgreSQL + streamlit services with health
  checks and named volumes

- Create tests/__init__.py, tests/test_rules.py, tests/test_api.py:
  71 tests covering all rules, the AMLRuleEngine class, and all API endpoints

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add GitHub Actions PR validation workflow (#2233)

* Add research and refactoring plan for win32metadata reinvestment

Add structured planning documents covering five workstreams:
1. Fix PR validation gates
2. Add regression testing
3. Fix WinmdGenerator MSBuild compatibility
4. Version-aligned packaging
5. Shift-left metadata definition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GitHub Actions PR validation workflow

Add a GitHub Actions workflow that mirrors the Azure Pipelines CI
pipeline, ensuring PR checks run reliably on every pull request.

Pipeline structure:
- 3 parallel scrape jobs via matrix strategy (x64, x86, arm64)
- 1 build/test/package job that assembles the winmd and runs the
  full test suite including CompareBinToLastRelease validation

Excludes code signing and NuGet push (CI-only concerns).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(frontend): build storybook intake experience

Complete implementation of the Verifesto Studios frontend — a
storybook-themed single-page intake form with premium design.

What's included:

- StoryBookCover: dark navy book cover with gold monogram, decorative
  inner frame, radial blue glow, ghost CTA button that animates to
  verity blue on hover with sliding arrow

- Book-open page transition: cover scales up and blurs out as welcome
  page fades up from below — feels like opening a hardcover

- WelcomePage: "Chapter One" editorial label, Fraunces display heading,
  verity accent, full-bleed "Turn the page" button

- ContactPage: "Chapter Two", 4 fields (name, email, phone optional,
  company optional), inline validation with soft warm error styling

- VisionPage: "Chapter Three", large textarea, live sentence counter
  with green ✓ when 3 sentences reached, NDA checkbox with animated
  tooltip (hover + tap)

- ReviewPage: "Final Chapter", clean summary cards with Edit links,
  spinning submit button with loading state, soft error display

- ConfirmationPage: success icon, personalised thank you, 3-step
  what-happens-next flow, return to cover link

- useIntakeForm composable: singleton reactive state, client-side
  validation for contact and vision, beginStory() for book-open
  transition, countSentences() helper

- style.css: page-forward, page-back, book-open CSS transitions,
  form-input/form-error/btn-primary/review-card shared classes,
  fadeInUp entrance animation, chapter-label typography

Build: ✓ vite build passes, 90kB JS / 25kB CSS gzipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(frontend): build complete storybook intake experience

- StoryBookCover: Landing with logo, 'Begin Your Story' CTA
- WelcomePage: Studio intro with warm, inviting copy
- ContactPage: Name, email, phone (opt), company (opt) with validation
- VisionPage: Textarea (min 3 sentences), NDA checkbox with tooltip
- ReviewPage: Summary with edit links, submit button with loading state
- ConfirmationPage: Success with next steps and personalized greeting
- useIntakeForm composable: Full form state, validation, API submission
- Smooth page-forward/page-back slide transitions (400ms cubic-bezier)
- Fade-in-up entrance animations on each page
- Fixed header with back button + progress dots
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Brand tokens: Fraunces + Inter typography, Verity Blue accent
- Premium form inputs with focus rings and error states

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Generated initial migration for Inquiry model
- Added .venv/ to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: scaffold monorepo with Vue 3 + Django

- Vue 3 + Vite + Tailwind 4 frontend scaffold
- Django 5 + DRF backend with intake app
- Inquiry model, serializer, API endpoint, admin config
- Brand tokens (Fraunces, Inter, Verity Blue palette)
- PostgreSQL support via dj-database-url
- CORS, WhiteNoise, gunicorn production-ready
- .gitignore, .env.example, README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix global search bar hidden behind sidebar when expanded

The expanded state used left: -400px to stretch leftward, but graph-area has
overflow: hidden so that portion was clipped, making the bar appear hidden.
Fixed by anchoring to left: 12px (stays within graph-area regardless of
sidebar width) and right: 190px (leaves room for the modules panel).

Also added margin-right: 8px to the collapsed magnifier so it sits with a
small breathing gap from the modules panel rather than snug against it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove record count badges from table view

The (5), (1), (2) counts on each table name label were not adding useful
information given the records are immediately visible below. Removing them
reduces visual noise and makes the label column more compact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 'Hide all terminal tables' persisted checkbox to depth panel

Adds a checkbox under the depth sliders that hides all terminal tables from
both the graph view and the table view. State is persisted like any other
user setting. Graph uses a new Simulation.setHiddenTableNames() filter that
is non-destructive (same approach as setDisplayDepth). Table view filters
them out of the rendered list.

commit.message:
translate: update pt-br/photography.md gallery

- Add new photo #29231673 at gallery start
- Update all alt texts to numeric photo ID format (#XXXXXXX)
- Remove photos #28292145 and #28289537
- Add photo #28302088

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Detect Cloudflare challenge pages and combine photo detail fetches

- is_valid_lomography_response now rejects pages with CF challenge
  markers (Just a moment, cf_chl_opt, etc.) so curl failures fall
  through to cloudscraper which can solve the challenges.
- Combine fetch_photo_cdn_url + fetch_photo_title into a single
  fetch_photo_details call, halving the number of requests per photo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Validate photo IDs in response before accepting fetch result

The Cloudflare challenge page on GH Actions contains lomography.com
references, so generic content validation passes. Instead, validate at
the parsing level: only accept a fetch if we actually find photo IDs in
the response. Both curl and cloudscraper are now tried on each attempt,
with retries and exponential backoff.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Lomography workflow failing silently on Cloudflare blocks

- Add content validation (is_valid_lomography_response) to detect when
  curl/cloudscraper returns a Cloudflare challenge page instead of real
  Lomography content. Previously the >1000 byte check passed for
  challenge pages, skipping the cloudscraper fallback entirely.
- When 0 photos are found from the profile page, exit with error code 1
  instead of silently reporting no new photos. This makes the workflow
  fail visibly so the issue is noticed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update cli/src/daemon/server.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: aatchison <8539707+aatchison@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Arron Atchison <arron.atchison@gmail.com>

Pixi support (#2)

* Fix missing submodule for UCE (#15)

* Fix bug in preprocessing of sparse vs non-sparse adata  (#14)

* Simplify installation instructions in README

Removed redundant instruction for downloading the repository.

* Remove unnecessary extra environment for gseapy access (covered by main env)

* Update setup.sh to comment flash-attn installation

Comment out flash-attn installation due to environment issues.

* Add pixi and nix flake configuration for local development

Add pixi.toml with conda-forge dependencies (Python 3.10, PyTorch, scanpy,
geneformer, etc.) and pip editable install of cellwhisperer + cellxgene fork.
Include flake.nix providing a NixOS FHS environment to run pixi.

* Update cellxgene submodule: Werkzeug 3.x fix, pandas 2.x, rename to cellxgene-cellwhisperer

Points to epigen/cellxgene commit 90345e3 which includes:
- Fix FlatBuffers bytes/bytearray compatibility with Werkzeug 3.x
- Require pandas>=2.0.1
- Rename package to cellxgene-cellwhisperer to avoid conflicts
- Skip Puppeteer download in client Makefile

* Fix torch.load compatibility with PyTorch 2.6+

PyTorch 2.6 changed torch.load default to weights_only=True, which rejects
checkpoints containing custom classes (e.g. TranscriptomeTextDualEncoderConfig).
Pass weights_only=False explicitly where needed, and monkey-patch torch.load in
load_from_checkpoint to handle Lightning checkpoint loading.

* Make scgpt imports lazy to support environments without scgpt

Move scgpt imports inside functions that use them so the package can run with
geneformer (default) without requiring scgpt to be installed. Also use string
comparison for foundation model class checks to avoid import dependency.

* Add pixi installation instructions to README

Add pixi as the top-most (recommended) installation option, keeping the
existing conda instructions as Option B.

* Fix missing gseapy in pixi

* Replace local LLaVA/Mixtral with API + lightweight local curation

- llava_annotate_clusters now uses the hosted CellWhisperer API instead of
  requiring a local LLaVA model and separate conda env
- Unified curate_llava_annotations rule replaces separate gpt4/mixtral rules:
  uses OpenAI GPT-4 if OPENAI_API_KEY is valid, falls back to local
  Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct via transformers
- Add cellwhisperer_api_url to config.yaml (used by API annotation rule)
- Upgrade transformers>=4.45 and add accelerate for local model support
- Add gseapy dependency

* Retain local LLaVA annotation rule as llava_annotate_clusters_local

API-based rule is preferred via ruleorder. The local rule can be
invoked explicitly with --forcerun llava_annotate_clusters_local
and requires the llava conda env + GPU.

* Update README with pixi commands and new curation fallback docs

* Add news section to landing page (Pixi support, Mila talk)

* Revert "Add news section to landing page (Pixi support, Mila talk)"

This reverts commit 76f405e4d7a21f793a1993e96c6d96cf4d97cfbd.

* Fix checkpoint loading for newer transformers and add frontend build step to README

* Simplify Pixi setup: remove Nix files, drop uni dep, add setup script (#25)

* Initial plan

* Address review comments: remove flake files, remove uni dep, simplify README with setup script

Co-authored-by: moritzschaefer <2714540+moritzschaefer@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: remove standalone-mcp module

The standalone MCP HTTP server plugin is no longer needed. Remove the
entire module, its CI steps, and its documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: include plugin-core classes on runtime classpath for buildSearchableOptions

IPP 2.x buildSearchableOptions creates its own fresh sandbox separate from
the prepareSandbox output. The doLast hack that copies plugin-core-classes.jar
into the regular sandbox is not visible to buildSearchableOptions, so any
configurable or service that references plugin-core types fails with
ClassNotFoundException / "Can't instantiate configurable".

Fix: add runtimeOnly(files(repackagePluginCore)) after the repackagePluginCore
task declaration in both standalone-mcp and plugin-experimental. This puts the
repackaged JAR (no plugin.xml) on the runtime configuration, which IPP uses
when setting up the buildSearchableOptions classpath — so ToolPermissionsConfigurable
and all other plugin-core-backed configurables can be loaded without error.

The existing doLast copy in prepareSandbox is kept as belt-and-suspenders for
the regular sandbox (used by runIde / deploy-to-ide).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: upgrade Gradle 8.13 → 9.4.1 and IntelliJ Platform Plugin 2.11.0 → 2.13.1

- Gradle 9.4.1 adds Java 26 support, non-class-based JVM tests, enhanced
  console progress bar
- IntelliJ Platform Plugin 2.13.1 fixes multiple issues and is the latest
  stable version compatible with Gradle 9.4.1
- Tested: Deploy to Main IDE runs successfully with this configuration
- CI workflows: add --build-cache flag to enable build caching at Gradle 9.4.1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: fix CI noise and security warnings

- ci.yml / release.yml: add FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true to
  silence Node.js 20 deprecation warnings (will be default June 2026)
- ci.yml / release.yml: add --quiet to all Gradle steps to suppress
  per-task progress output; add --silent to npm ci steps
- build.gradle.kts: update IntelliJ Platform Plugin 2.11.0 → 2.13.1
  to silence "outdated" warning during initialization
- standalone-mcp/build.gradle.kts: remove deprecated instrumentationTools()
  call (no longer necessary)
- js-tests/package.json: update happy-dom ^17.0.0 → ^20.0.0 to fix
  critical vulnerability (GHSA-37j7-fg3j-429f, VM context escape/RCE)
- AgentProfile.java: make getBinaryName() return "" instead of null
  when field is unset, keeping @NotNull contract honest
- AuthLoginService.kt: replace null-coalescing Elvis (?:) with
  .ifEmpty {} since binaryName is @NotNull (eliminates Kotlin warning)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add CODEX_APP_SERVER transport type and GenericSettings project constructor

- TransportType: add CODEX_APP_SERVER enum value used by CodexAppServerClient
- GenericSettings: add 2-arg constructor (prefix, project) for project-scoped
  settings storage; refactor all PropertiesComponent.getInstance() calls to use
  the new getProperties() helper that delegates to project-level or app-level storage

Fixes CI compilation errors:
  - CodexAppServerClient.java:131 - cannot find symbol TransportType.CODEX_APP_SERVER
  - ActiveAgentManager.java:298 - GenericSettings constructor arg count mismatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Codex settings page and fix binary PATH detection

- Create CodexClientConfigurable: shows binary/auth status, binary path
  override, "Sign In" and "Sign In — headless (--device-auth)" buttons
  for remote/SSH environments
- Register it in plugin.xml under the Agents group
- Fix CodexAppServerClient.resolveBinary(): replace custom findOnPath()
  (used only System.getenv("PATH")) with BinaryDetector.findBinaryPath()
  which spawns a login shell — correctly finds npm global installs and
  nvm-managed Node binaries that are not on the JVM's inherited PATH

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): fix plugin-experimental build failure and update workflows

- Fix RunInspectionsTool.java: add missing Computable import and
  explicit (Computable<T>) casts to resolve runReadAction ambiguity
  (javac saw Computable<T> vs ThrowableComputable<T,E> as ambiguous)
- Update ci.yml and release.yml: downgrade actions to stable versions
  (checkout@v4, setup-java@v4, setup-gradle@v4) and disable Gradle
  caching (cache-disabled: true)
- Add :plugin-experimental:buildPlugin to CI build step so experimental
  compilation errors are caught before merge

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(codex): add CodexAppServerClient and wire into agent registry

- Add CodexAppServerClient: bidirectional JSON-RPC 2.0 client for
  `codex app-server`, communicating via stdio JSONL transport
- Add CodexCredentials: reads ~/.codex/auth.json to check login state
- Register CODEX_PROFILE_ID in AgentProfileManager (ensureDefaults +
  createDefaultProfile switch)
- Wire CodexAppServerClient into ActiveAgentManager.start() alongside
  AnthropicDirectClient and ClaudeCliClient branches
- Fix BannerLevel.INFO → WARNING (INFO variant does not exist)
- Fix InterruptedException handling: call Thread.currentThread().interrupt()
- Extract duplicate string literals to constants (F_COMMAND, F_REASONING,
  AGENTS_MD)
- Remove unused field F_ITEM_ID
- Document empty catch blocks in closeQuietly/startStderrDrainer
- Make isHealthy() check process liveness in addition to connected flag
- Extract processLine() from startReaderThread to reduce nesting
- Extract handleResponse/completeResponseFuture from dispatchMessage
  to reduce cognitive complexity
- Add default case to item type switch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add null check for ExpiresAt in Housekeeping expiry condition

The 'less' function fails when ExpiresAt is null (not just empty string).
Added explicit null check before the date comparison.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: convert HousekeepingFlow to HttpRequest + Compose config

- 6 actions converted (3 GetItems, 1 PatchItem, 2 DeleteItem → HttpRequest)
- Added Compose:_Config_SiteURL for portable site URL
- Rebuilt PowerClaw_Solution.zip with all 4 flows
- Updated SETUP.md to include Housekeeping in Configure Flows step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace deprecated ReadAction.compute/run with ApplicationManager.runReadAction

Replace all usages of deprecated ReadAction.compute() and ReadAction.run()
with ApplicationManager.getApplication().runReadAction() across plugin-core
and plugin-experimental. These APIs are deprecated in IntelliJ 2026.1 (IU-261).

Also replace deprecated JBPopupFactory.createListPopupBuilder(JBList) with
createPopupChooserBuilder(List) in ChatToolWindowContent.kt, using the new
Consumer<T> callback instead of Runnable.

Document intentional use of experimental/scheduled-for-removal APIs in
PlatformApiCompat where no non-experimental alternatives exist:
- LafManager.getInstalledThemes() (@ApiStatus.Experimental, no alternative)
- DataPack via DataPackChangeListener (scheduled-for-removal, but using the
  internal DATA_PACK_REFRESH alternative would cause a build FAILURE)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: pre-create ~/.pluginVerifier/extracted-plugins before verifyPlugin

Plugin verifier 1.401 crashes with NoSuchFileException when this
directory doesn't exist (it tries to delete it unconditionally).
gradle/actions/setup-gradle v6 no longer caches ~/.pluginVerifier,
exposing the bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Jenkins GROUP_SCRATCH Storage Management (#1470)

* update jenkins runscripts to purge old files

* save only 14 most recently failed as well

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Revert default engineering length metric formatting to meters (#9120)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make current_apy time-weighted

- Store activation_timestamp when transitioning vault to Active state
- Calculate APY using formula: (yield / assets) * (seconds_per_year / elapsed_seconds) * 10000
- Handle edge cases: zero elapsed time, zero assets, zero yield
- Use checked arithmetic to prevent overflow on intermediate values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Address PR #178 review feedback

- Add LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER config flag (independent of USE_LLM) so report
  generation is opt-in rather than an implicit side-effect of enabling LLMs
- Add --llm-report CLI flag wired to LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER
- Gate LLMReporter.generate() on USE_LLM AND LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER
- Gate fuzzer.py LLMReporter call behind config.LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER check
- Add _MAX_FINDINGS_IN_REPORT (50) and _MAX_SUMMARY_CHARS (1500) caps to
  prevent context-limit issues on large runs
- Fix _extract_json_from_text to validate isinstance(parsed, dict) and raise
  ValueError for non-object JSON (list/bool/etc.) so retry loop can correct it
- Add unit tests for LLMReporter: disabled flags, no detections, success path,
  LLM failure handling, truncation, and findings cap (10 new tests)
- Update README --help block and --use-llm description to reflect broader
  LLM scope (inference + endpoint classification + IDOR chain classification)
- Clarify USE_LLM comment in config.py to list all LLM-driven features

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: pySDC is a Python implementation of the spectral deferred correction (SDC) approach and its flavors, esp. the multilevel extension MLSDC and PFASST.

commit.message:
docs: update chat artifact paths to reflect generated resource location

The chat build artifacts (chat-components.js, chat.css) are no longer
stored in src/main/resources/chat/ — they're generated into
build/generated/resources/chat-ui/chat/ by the buildChatUi task.

Update copilot-instructions.md and chat-ui/README.md to reference the
correct generated path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
